title: Mouth breathing
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.
examination.rationale: The exact examination section supports clinician observation of mouth breathing and general, nasal, oral, throat, septal, secretion, crusting, or polyp findings without auto-populating them.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-performed general inspection, mouth-breathing observation, airflow assessment, anterior rhinoscopy, secretions, septal appearance, polyps, crusting, throat, and postnasal-drip documentation.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)

exact_section.heading: Sections 8.1–8.3 — visual, nasal, oral, and upper-airway examination

investigations.text: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.
investigations.rationale: The exact investigation context supports review of already available specialist information and a clinician-entered plan or follow-up without recommending testing or treatment.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports recording clinician-selected allergy testing and already available specialist or imaging results; routine radiology and autonomous testing are not mapped.

source.title: BSACI guideline for the diagnosis and management of allergic and non-allergic rhinitis (Revised Edition 2017; First edition 2007)

exact_section.heading: Sections 9.1–9.8 — allergy testing and investigation context

medication.text: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.
medication.rationale: The exact rhinitis history section directly supports onset, nasal obstruction, mouth breathing, sleep or snoring impact, associated nasal, eye or lower-airway features, relevant negatives, and contextual triggers without diagnosing rhinitis or adenoid disease.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting onset, duration, triggers, rhinorrhoea, obstruction, sneezing, itching, crusting, eye and lower-airway symptoms, sleep, snoring, mouth breathing, family, social, occupational, and medicine context without assigning rhinitis subtype.

source_limitations: Age, duration, daytime or sleep pattern, obstruction, snoring, apnoea, allergy, adenoid, septum, dental and speech impact, examination, testing, diagnosis, treatment, and referral remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
